model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Houston holds a commanding ~10.4-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Saint Mary's (KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55 vs. KenPom #24, AdjEM +23.11; Torvik #5 vs. #25), anchored by the 4th-best defense in the country (AdjDE 91.0) that is perfectly positioned to neutralize a Gaels offense ranked only 43rd-48th nationally — the most critical matchup asymmetry in this game. While Saint Mary's elite defense (22nd nationally) is a genuine weapon under Randy Bennett, Houston's offense ranked 13th-16th nationally is the toughest assignment the Gaels have faced all season, far exceeding anything the WCC schedule provided. Houston's Big 12 battle-testing — including a conference tournament final appearance against #3 Arizona — reflects a level of schedule-strength validation that Saint Mary's WCC résumé cannot match, and the Cougars are playing with home-state crowd support near campus. The combination of Houston's superior two-way efficiency, elite defensive profile, Big 12 battle-hardening, and the historical ~75% win rate for 2-seeds over 7-seeds make the Cougars a clear and confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Saint Mary's vs Houston — Round of 32 South Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Houston is a dominant top-5 national program with a ~10.4-point efficiency advantage over Saint Mary's, the 4th-best defense in the country, and Big 12 battle-testing that far exceeds the WCC schedule. Saint Mary's elite defense is their best weapon but is insufficient to overcome the two-way excellence Houston brings, particularly given the Gaels' offense ranked only 43rd-48th nationally running into the nation's 4th-best defense.
evidence: Houston: KenPom #5 (AdjEM +33.55, AdjO 124.6 [16th], AdjD 91.0 [4th]), Torvik #5 (AdjOE 125.1 [13th], AdjDE 91.85 [4th]), 28-5 record, Big 12. Saint Mary's: KenPom #24 (AdjEM +23.11, AdjO 120.3 [43rd], AdjD 97.2 [22nd]), Torvik #25 (AdjOE 119.4 [48th], AdjDE 97.6 [22nd]), 27-5 record, WCC. Efficiency gap: ~10.4 KenPom points.

Houston seeded 2nd in the South, playing near home. Saint Mary's seeded 7th, advanced past Texas A&M. No significant injury concerns flagged for Houston with LJ Cryer and Emanuel Sharp both active contributors throughout the season.

2-seeds defeat 7-seeds approximately 75% of the time historically. The ~10-point efficiency gap is well above the threshold that typically produces upsets. Saint Mary's WCC schedule leaves them underprepared for Houston's two-way excellence at the national top-5 level.

By Eddie Maisonet , Sports Editor March 15, 2026 The Houston Cougars will take their talents north of the Red River for their opening round game in the 2026 NCAA tournament. Icon Sportswire/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images The Houston Cougars now know their path to win the school's first-ever NCAA men's basketball championship. On Sunday, 68 schools learned of their selection, seeding and schedule for the 2026 edition of a tournament affectionately known as March Madness. The Cougars won't have to travel too far to start things off, and if things go according to plan, one or two defacto home games could be in order. Advertisement Article continues below this ad The Cougars earned a No. 2 seed in the South Region of the tournament, and they will face the No. 15 seed Idaho Vandals on March 19 at Paycom Center in Oklahoma City, Okla. For Cougars head coach Kelvin Sampson, playing in Oklahoma should serve as a homecoming of sorts. Sampson coached the Oklahoma Sooners for 12 seasons, earning a Final Four appearance in 2001. If the Cougars can get past the Vandals in the opening round, a second-round matchup looms between the winner of No. 7 seed St. Mary's vs. in-state rival and No. 10 seed Texas A&M on March 21.
